body_txt: In a setting where there are demons and angels or the equivalent forces of cosmic evil and good, evil will go out and do things such as raze villages, kill people and destroy countrysides . Meanwhile, God will only exist as a symbolic force to inspire heroes. Either that or you'll have the Powers That Be incapable of or unwilling to stop a God of Evil . Either way they won't have a hands-on approach. This is to prevent the feeling of Deus ex Machina but In-Universe it doesn't work very well , the message seeming to be that Good Is Boring , Good Is Impotent and Being Good Sucks but Evil Is Cool , Evil Tastes Good and Evil Is Easy . The explanation for this is usually that God solving people's problems would prevent free will... but if you think about it monsters and such coming to wreak havoc sure is screwing around with free will and the least the angels could do is get off their bums and keep a damn balance . In the worst case-scenarios this can lead to characters enacting some form of revenge for such ignorance by starting a Rage Against the Heavens , undergoing a Faith–Heel Turn , becoming a Nay-Theist or choosing to try Fighting Your God to Kill the God ; alternatively the one actively forsaking their God Job can be branded a Loser Deity . Another explanation is that good "plays by the rules" and both sides promised not to interfere... but in practice evil lies and figures losing a few minions is a cheap price for the unopposed everywhere else . Inevitably the angels and such only come once mortals have done all the hard work or when things have gotten so bad only a Deus ex Machina could possibly fix things. Any proactive good-aligned celestial beings will somehow inevitably be worse than the basest villains in practice if not intent , intent as well as practice or quickly done away with . It may also be that it's actually more acceptable to show forces of evil than forces of good. For instance you have shows like Reaper where The Devil is a main character; conversely there was Joan of Arcadia . Guess which one the Moral Guardians brought the hammer down on? And some religions dislike the display of images of their deities. It might also be more about the fact that if you have good-mortals-plus-angels vs. evil-mortals-plus-demons then the battle is too even, so to have the good mortals fighting against impossible odds the angels have to sit it out because underdogs are who the audience is supposed to root for . This trope is influenced by changes in attitudes towards Real Life conflict in the 20th century. Just declaring war is something the "bad guys" did while inspiring resistance forces but letting fights ultimately be up to locals is more the "good guys" style. (Yes, there's quite a lot of asterisks and quotation marks to this in the real world.) See also Alien Non-Interference Clause , Almighty Idiot , All-Powerful Bystander , Amnesiac God , Being God Is Hard , Disabled Deity , Divine Misfile , God Is Flawed , God Is Inept , God Is Neutral , God Is Displeased , Godly Sidestep , God's Hands Are Tied , Gods Need Prayer Badly , Incredibly Inconvenient Deity , Powers That Be , Have You Seen My God? , Pieces of God , Omniscient Morality License , Not So Omniscient After All , Reasoning with God , Offerings to the Gods , The Watcher , Not Quite the Almighty , Neglectful Precursors . If the good deities aren't just lazy but flat out don't exist or are absent that's Devil, but No God or God Is Dead . Compare Lazy Boss for lazy authority figures in general. Should the deities involved be unpleasant or incredibly morally and ethically complex see Trickster God , Jackass Genie , Jerkass Gods and Abusive Precursors . If this trope is Played With enough to possibly be sent to its Logical Extreme or flipped entirely see Ascended Demon , The Anti-God , Demiurge Archetype , Destroyer Deity , God of Darkness , God of Chaos , Holy Is Not Safe , Satan Is Good , Mad God , Order Is Not Good , God of Evil , God Is Evil , Fallen Cupid , Fallen Angel , Both Order and Chaos are Dangerous and God and Satan Are Both Jerks . The trope name comes from the film The Gods Must Be Crazy . If this trope is averted (i.e. gods take more active roles in fighting evil) see Bolt of Divine Retribution , Bargain with Heaven , Benevolent Genie , Guardian Angel , Pals with Jesus , Patron God , Divine Assistance , Divine Conflict , Divine Intervention , Divine Punishment , Gaia's Vengeance , God Was My Co-Pilot , Emissary from the Divine , Order Versus Chaos , Good Versus Evil , Heaven Versus Hell , Hell Invades Heaven , Healer God , God of Light , God of Order , Light Is Good , God of Good and God Is Good . The villainous version of this is Orcus on His Throne . Compare In Mysterious Ways where it's left unclear whether or not this trope is in effect . This might be the reason a work falls into Class 3 on the Sliding Scale of Divine Intervention . For the unrelated but similar-sounding trope see The Guards Must Be Crazy .
